title: Känguru der Mathematik 2016 — Felix (Pré-Écolier, Grades 1-2)
source_organization: Känguru der Mathematik (Austria) / Kangourou sans Frontières
disclaimer: Official Math Kangaroo past paper (kaenguru.at, via matematica.pt). For personal educational practice only.
answer_source: Official 2016_Answers.pdf answer table (Felix row), read and verified.

questions.question_text: How many ropes can you see in this picture?
questions.solution: Each rope has two ends. Follow the tangled lines from end to end; there are 3 separate ropes.
solution_steps: Each rope has exactly two loose ends.
Trace each line from one end to its other end.
There are 3 separate ropes.

questions.question_text: In a cave there live 1 starfish, 2 seahorses and 3 turtles. They are visited by 3 starfish, 4 turtles and 5 seahorses. How many animals are in the cave now?
questions.solution: Already there: 1 + 2 + 3 = 6 animals. Visitors: 3 + 4 + 5 = 12 animals. Altogether 6 + 12 = 18.
solution_steps: Animals living there: 1 + 2 + 3 = 6.
Visiting animals: 3 + 4 + 5 = 12.

questions.question_text: Which point in the labyrinth can we get to, starting at point O?
questions.solution: Follow the open passages from the centre O without crossing a wall. The only point you can reach this way is C.
solution_steps: Start at O and follow passages with no wall blocking them.
Most points are walled off from O.
Only point C can be reached.

questions.question_text: Ten friends go to Robert's birthday party. Six of them are girls. How many boys in total are at the party?
questions.solution: Of the 10 friends, 10 − 6 = 4 are boys. Robert is a boy too and is at his own party, so there are 4 + 1 = 5 boys in total.
solution_steps: Boys among the 10 friends: 10 − 6 = 4.
Robert himself is also a boy at the party.
Total boys = 4 + 1 = 5.

questions.question_text: Johannes hands out flyers to the houses with numbers 15 to 47. How many houses get a flyer?
questions.solution: Count every house from 15 to 47 inclusive: 47 − 15 + 1 = 33 houses.
solution_steps: Houses run from 15 to 47, both included.
That is 33 houses.

questions.question_text: Max has 10 dice. Which one of the following solids can he build with them?
questions.solution: Count the cubes in each solid, including hidden ones. Only solid A is made of exactly 10 cubes.
solution_steps: Count the small cubes in each solid, hidden ones too.
Find the one that uses exactly 10.
That is solid A.

questions.question_text: Lisa puts 6 white and brown eggs into a 2-by-3 box. Brown eggs are not allowed to touch each other. What is the maximum number of brown eggs she can place?
questions.solution: Place brown eggs so that no two are side by side. Using a checkerboard arrangement in the 2×3 box fits 3 brown eggs without any touching.
solution_steps: No two brown eggs may sit next to each other.
Put them on alternating spaces, like a checkerboard.
That fits 3 brown eggs.

questions.question_text: Grandma calls her cat and all her chickens. Soon 20 legs are running towards her. How many chickens does Grandma have?
questions.solution: The cat has 4 legs, leaving 20 − 4 = 16 legs for the chickens. Each chicken has 2 legs, so 16 ÷ 2 = 8 chickens.
solution_steps: The cat uses 4 legs, so chickens have 20 − 4 = 16 legs.
Each chicken has 2 legs.
16 ÷ 2 = 8 chickens.

questions.question_text: A house has 12 rooms, each with two windows and one light. Both windows are lit only when that room's light is on. Last evening 18 windows were lit. In how many rooms was the light off?
questions.solution: Each lit room shows 2 lit windows, so 18 ÷ 2 = 9 rooms had their light on. The light was off in 12 − 9 = 3 rooms.
solution_steps: Lit windows come in pairs (2 per lit room).
18 ÷ 2 = 9 rooms had the light on.
Light off in 12 − 9 = 3 rooms.

questions.question_text: Together Paul and Josef are 12 years old. How old will they both be together in four years' time?
questions.solution: In four years each boy is 4 years older, so together they gain 4 + 4 = 8 years. 12 + 8 = 20.
solution_steps: Each boy ages 4 years.
Together that adds 4 + 4 = 8 years.

questions.question_text: Gerda walks along the road and writes down the letters she sees on her right-hand side. Which word is formed as she walks from point 1 to point 2?
questions.solution: Walking from 1 to 2, the letters on Gerda's right come in the order K, N, A, O, spelling KNAO.
solution_steps: Walk the path from point 1 toward point 2.
Note only the letters on the right-hand side, in order.
They read K, N, A, O = KNAO.

questions.question_text: Konrad has many cardboard pieces all shaped like an L-tromino (three squares). Which of the shapes below can he NOT make out of these pieces?
questions.solution: Each piece covers 3 squares, so a shape can only be built if it can be split into L-trominoes. Shape D cannot be divided into these pieces, so it cannot be made.
solution_steps: Each piece covers exactly 3 squares in an L shape.
Try to cut each shape into such L-pieces.
Only shape D cannot be split this way.

questions.question_text: Five sparrows sit on a rope, some looking left and some looking right. Each sparrow whistles as many times as the number of sparrows it can see in front of it. The third sparrow whistles exactly twice. How often do all sparrows whistle altogether?
questions.solution: Each sparrow sees only the sparrows ahead in the way it faces. Using the directions shown (and the clue that the third sees 2), adding up what every sparrow sees gives 10 whistles in total.
solution_steps: A sparrow counts only the sparrows ahead in its facing direction.
The third sparrow facing its way sees 2 (matching the clue).
Adding each sparrow's count gives 10 whistles.

questions.question_text: Each of five ladybirds sits on a flower where: (1) the difference between the points on its two wings equals the number of leaves on the stem, and (2) the total points on its wings equals the number of petals. Which flower is left without a ladybird?
questions.solution: For each ladybird work out its total points (must equal the petals) and its wing difference (must equal the leaves), then match it to a flower. Flower E matches none of the five ladybirds, so it stays empty.
solution_steps: For each ladybird, find its total points and its wing difference.
Match it to the flower whose petals = total and leaves = difference.
Flower E has no matching ladybird.

questions.question_text: A cube is shown in two positions. Its six sides have different patterns. Which side is opposite to the diamond-in-a-crossed-square side?
questions.solution: Using the two views, find which faces are next to the crossed-diamond face. The face that never appears beside it is the small-square face, option C, so it is opposite.
solution_steps: From both views, list the faces neighbouring the crossed-diamond face.
The opposite face is the one never seen next to it.
That is option C.
